claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my child Alex, who is 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex!

Now I have a few more questions to determine your eligibility:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Just a couple more questions:

**Is Alex in any of the following situations?**
- In hospital
- In care (looked after by a local council)
- In residential accommodation

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any of those situations. They're just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

✅ You live in the UK
✅ Alex is under 16 years old (aged 6)
✅ Alex lives with you, meaning you're responsible for them
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
✅ Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. Please note that only one person can receive Child Benefit for a child, so if your circumstances change in the future, you may need to notify HMRC.

Also, be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit Tax Charge.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under the age of 16. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, meaning the claimant is responsible for Alex. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. All the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They live in the UK, are responsible for a child under 16 (Alex, aged 6) who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child. There are no complicating factors such as the child being in hospital or care.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old, which is under the age of 16. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, meaning the claimant is responsible for Alex. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. All the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They live in the UK, are responsible for a child under 16 (Alex, aged 6) who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child. There are no complicating factors such as the child being in hospital or care.
